root_text.an6.43:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an6.43:1.2: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.an6.43:1.3: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an6.43:1.5: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an6.43:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ yena pubbārāmo migāramātupāsādo tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.an6.43:2.2: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an6.43:2.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an6.43:2.5: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ yena pubbakoṭṭhako tenupasaṅkami gattāni parisiñcituṁ.
root_text.an6.43:2.6: Pubbakoṭṭhake gattāni parisiñcitvā paccuttaritvā ekacīvaro aṭṭhāsi gattāni pubbāpayamāno.
root_text.an6.43:3.1: Tena kho pana samayena rañño pasenadissa kosalassa seto nāma nāgo mahātūriyatāḷitavāditena pubbakoṭṭhakā paccuttarati.
root_text.an6.43:3.3: “abhirūpo vata bho rañño nāgo; dassanīyo vata bho rañño nāgo; pāsādiko vata, bho, rañño nāgo, kāyupapanno vata bho rañño nāgo”ti.
root_text.an6.43:3.4: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā udāyī bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.43:3.5: “hatthimeva nu kho, bhante, mahantaṁ brahantaṁ kāyupapannaṁ jano disvā evamāha:
root_text.an6.43:3.6: ‘nāgo vata bho nāgo’ti, udāhu aññampi kañci mahantaṁ brahantaṁ kāyupapannaṁ jano disvā evamāha: ‘nāgo vata bho nāgo’”ti?
root_text.an6.43:3.7: “Hatthimpi kho, udāyi, mahantaṁ brahantaṁ kāyupapannaṁ jano disvā evamāha:
root_text.an6.43:3.13: manussampi kho, udāyi, mahantaṁ brahantaṁ kāyupapannaṁ jano disvā evamāha:
root_text.an6.43:3.15: Api ca, udāyi, yo sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya āguṁ na karoti kāyena vācāya manasā, tamahaṁ ‘nāgo’ti brūmī”ti.
root_text.an6.43:4.3: api ca, udāyi, yo sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya āguṁ na karoti kāyena vācāya manasā, tamahaṁ ‘nāgo’ti brūmīti.
root_text.an6.43:4.4: Idañca panāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā subhāsitaṁ imāhi gāthāhi anumodāmi—

translation_text.an6.43:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an6.43:1.2: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.an6.43:1.3: Then, after the meal, on his return from almsround, he addressed Venerable Ānanda,
translation_text.an6.43:1.4: “Come, Ānanda, let’s go to the stilt longhouse of Migāra’s mother in the Eastern Monastery for the day’s meditation.”
translation_text.an6.43:2.1: So the Buddha went with Ānanda to the Eastern Monastery.
translation_text.an6.43:2.2: In the late afternoon the Buddha came out of retreat and addressed Ānanda,
translation_text.an6.43:2.3: “Come, Ānanda, let’s go to the eastern gate to bathe.”
translation_text.an6.43:2.5: So the Buddha went with Ānanda to the eastern gate to bathe.
translation_text.an6.43:2.6: When he had bathed and emerged from the water he stood in one robe drying his limbs.
translation_text.an6.43:3.1: Now, at that time King Pasenadi had a giant bull elephant called “White”. It emerged from the eastern gate to the beating and playing of musical instruments.
translation_text.an6.43:3.2: When people saw it they said,
translation_text.an6.43:3.3: “The royal giant is so handsome! The royal giant is so good-looking! The royal giant is so lovely! The royal giant has such a huge body!”
translation_text.an6.43:3.4: When they said this, Venerable Udāyī said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.43:3.5: “Sir, is it only when they see elephants with such a huge, formidable body that people say:
translation_text.an6.43:3.6: ‘A giant, such a giant’? Or do they say it when they see any other creatures with huge, formidable bodies?”
translation_text.an6.43:3.7: “Udāyī, when they see elephants with such a huge, formidable body people say:
translation_text.an6.43:3.9: And also when they see a horse with a huge, formidable body …
translation_text.an6.43:3.10: When they see a bull with a huge, formidable body …
translation_text.an6.43:3.11: When they see a serpent with a huge, formidable body …
translation_text.an6.43:3.12: When they see a tree with a huge, formidable body …
translation_text.an6.43:3.13: And when they see a human being with such a huge, formidable body people say:
translation_text.an6.43:3.15: But Udāyī, one who does nothing monstrous by way of body, speech, and mind is who I call a ‘giant’ in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.”
translation_text.an6.43:4.2: How well said this was by the Buddha:
translation_text.an6.43:4.3: ‘But Udāyī, one who does nothing monstrous by way of body, speech, and mind is who I call a “giant” in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.’
translation_text.an6.43:4.4: And I celebrate the well-spoken words of the Buddha with these verses:
translation_text.an6.43:6.4: so I’ve heard from the perfected one.
translation_text.an6.43:7.4: he’s freed like lustrous gold from stone.
translation_text.an6.43:8.2: like the Himalaya beside other mountains.
translation_text.an6.43:9.1: I shall extol the giant for you,
translation_text.an6.43:9.4: are two feet of the giant.
translation_text.an6.43:11.1: Mindfulness is his neck, his head is wisdom—
translation_text.an6.43:11.3: His belly is the sacred hearth of the Dhamma,
translation_text.an6.43:12.3: The giant is serene when walking,
translation_text.an6.43:12.4: the giant is serene when standing,
translation_text.an6.43:13.1: the giant is serene when lying down,
translation_text.an6.43:13.2: and when sitting, the giant is serene.
translation_text.an6.43:13.4: this is the accomplishment of the giant.
translation_text.an6.43:14.3: When he gets food and clothes,
translation_text.an6.43:16.3: sprouts in water and grows there,
translation_text.an6.43:16.4: but water does not stick to it.
translation_text.an6.43:17.1: Just so the Buddha is born in the world,
translation_text.an6.43:17.3: but the world does not stick to him,
translation_text.an6.43:17.4: as water does not stick to the lotus.
translation_text.an6.43:18.2: dies down when the fuel runs out.
translation_text.an6.43:18.3: When the coals have gone out
translation_text.an6.43:19.1: This simile is taught by the discerning
translation_text.an6.43:19.4: what the giant taught the giant.
translation_text.an6.43:20.1: Free of greed, free of hate,
translation_text.an6.43:20.3: the giant, giving up his body,
translation_text.an6.43:20.4: being undefiled, will be fully quenched.”

variant_text.an6.43:3.5: mahantaṁ brahantaṁ → mahantaṁ bruhantaṁ (bj); mahattaṁ brahmattaṁ (mr)
variant_text.an6.43:16.3: Nupalippati → na upalippati (bj, cck, km, pts1ed); na upalimpati (sya1ed, sya2ed); nupalimpati (mr)
variant_text.an6.43:18.1: Mahāginīva jalito → mahāggini pajjalito (bj, sya-all, km); mahāgini pajjalito (pts1ed)
